Turn 9 ‚ÄĒ degree 14 dies; the residual tower is clear through I16 I re-read the three current attachments only. The original question is still the over-G isomorphism problem for the two quotients defined by rS,rH, with the required condition ŌÄH‚ąėő¶=ŌÄS.The prior sweep had reduced the only surviving obstruction to the residual Ef, mod-2, s=1, section-twisted Magnus equationS(Y0,Y1,q)=(Y1,Y1q)‚ąí1Y1‚ąí2Y03Y0q,X=1+A,Y=1+B,with target X2=1+A2, and Turn 8 left degree 14 as the next square-cokernel layerL14/im‚ąā13‚ČÖL7[2].

The result of this turn is:The¬†degree-14¬†residual¬†obstruction¬†also¬†dies.More precisely, there is a residual section-twisted lift satisfyingS(Y0,Y1,q)‚Č°X2 (modI15),and then the odd-layer argument from Turn 6 clears degree 15, givingS(Y0,Y1,q)‚Č°X2 (modI16).The computation was not a fixed-tail test. I used the same restricted Lie correction machine as Turns 7‚Äď8: actual group representatives generated recursively by commutators and squares, so the forced Bockstein tails are included rather than suppressed. This matters because Turn 8 already showed that a na√Įve fixed-gauge test can miss square directions at degree 12.

Let L‚ąô be the free restricted Lie algebra on A,B inside F2‚ü®‚ü®A,B‚ü©‚ü©, and let‚ąān:Ln‚äēLn‚ÜíLn+1,(W,Q)‚Ü¶[A,W]+[B,Q],with [U,V]=UV+VU in characteristic 2. The relevant dimensions aredimL7=18,dimL12=346,dimL13=630,dimL14=1179.

The boundary ranks at the new layer are rank‚ąā12=630,so degree 13 is fully covered, and rank‚ąā13=1161.

Hence dim(L14/im‚ąā13)=1179‚ąí1161=18=dimL7,as expected:L14/im‚ąā13‚ČÖL7[2].

Now take the full affine correction spaceV‚Č§12=d=8‚®Ā12(Ld‚äēLd),where the two copies correspond to corrections placed on Y1 and q. Its dimension isdimV‚Č§12=2(36+56+105+186+346)=1458.LetD‚Č§13:V‚Č§12‚ÜíT9‚äēT10‚äēT11‚äēT12‚äēT13 be the linearized residual-tail map through degree 13. Since all these corrections begin in degree at least 8, products of two corrections begin in degree at least 16. Therefore the correction problem is genuinely linear through degree 14; no hidden quadratic Maurer‚ÄďCartan terms can enter before degree 16.

The rank computation gives rankD‚Č§13=1323=56+105+186+346+630,so dimkerD‚Č§13=1458‚ąí1323=135.

The induced degree-14 square-tail map isőļ14:kerD‚Č§13‚ü∂L14/im‚ąā13‚ČÖL7[2].

The rank is rankőļ14=18.ThuskerD‚Č§13‚Ü†L7[2].So every possible degree-14 square-cokernel class can be moved by already-invisible affine correction freedom. There is no degree-14 obstruction.For the actual residual tail, I then used the full correction spaceV‚Č§13=d=8‚®Ā13(Ld‚äēLd),against homogeneous degrees 9,‚Ä¶,14. HeredimV‚Č§13=2(36+56+105+186+346+630)=2718.

The computed rank of the tail map is 2502=56+105+186+346+630+1179=n=9‚ąĎ14dimLn,and the actual residual vectorS(Y0(8),Y1(8),q(8))‚ąíX2 through degrees 9,‚Ä¶,14 lies in its image. One row-reduced solution uses 1232 correction coefficients, so writing the witness out would obscure the structure rather than clarify it. The rank-and-membership statement is the useful information.Therefore there are actual restricted group representatives and a section correction such thatS(Y0(14),Y1(14),q(14))‚Č°X2 (modI15).Finally, degree 15 is odd. Turn 6‚Äôs formal primitive-boundary argument applies: once degree 14 is solved, any first residual degree-15 tail is primitive and lies inL15=[A,L14]+[B,L14].

So a degree-14 correction clears it, givingS(Y0,Y1,q)‚Č°X2 (modI16).

This does not yet proveőďS‚ČÖGőďH.But Turn 9 closes the whole linear window after the Turn-5/Turn-6 I9 base lift: because all later corrections start in degree 8, the action stays linear through degree 15. The next genuinely new layer is degree 16, where products of two degree-8 corrections first appear and the next square quotient isL16/im‚ąā15‚ČÖL8[2],dimL8=36.So degree 16, unlike degree 14, is a genuinely nonlinear 8+8 Maurer‚ÄďCartan layer.

ESTABLISHED: The degree-14 residual square obstruction vanishes. More strongly,kerD‚Č§13‚Ü†L14/im‚ąā13‚ČÖL7[2],with rank 18, and the actual residual equation is solvable through I15. The odd-layer argument then clears degree 15, so the residual congruence is clear through I16.

GAP: This is still not a full construction of an over-G isomorphism őďS‚ČÖGőďH. The next layer, degree 16, is the first place after the I9 base lift where correction‚Äďcorrection products of degree 8+8 enter.

NEXT: Build the degree-16 nonlinear Maurer‚ÄďCartan equation, including the quadratic interaction of degree-8 affine-kernel corrections, and test whether the resulting affine/nonlinear freedom killsL16/im‚ąā15‚ČÖL8[2].[DEG14-CLEARED-LINEAR-WINDOW-TO-I16]
